hints: E[(AвӮҒвҲ’ДҖ)ВІ]=Var(AвӮҒвҲ’ДҖ)+(E[AвӮҒвҲ’ДҖ])ВІ.
E[AвӮҒ]=1, ОјМ„=10 вҮ’ mean part (вҲ’9)ВІ=81; AвӮҒвҲ’ДҖ=(9/10)AвӮҒвҲ’(1/10)ОЈ_{iвүҘ2}A_i.
solution: $\mathbb{E}[(A_1-\bar A)^2]=\mathrm{Var}(A_1-\bar A)+(\mathbb{E}[A_1-\bar A])^2$. Here $\mathbb{E}[A_1]=1$, $\bar\mu=\tfrac1{10}\sum(2i-1)=10$, so the mean term is $(1-10)^2=81$. Writing $A_1-\bar A=\tfrac9{10}A_1-\tfrac1{10}\sum_{i=2}^{10}A_i$ with independent variance-$100$ terms: $\mathrm{Var}=\tfrac{81}{100}(100)+\tfrac{9}{100}(100)=90$. Total $=90+81=171.$

hints: You have 7- and 11-pint vessels and want exactly 2 pints, minimizing fill/empty/pour transactions.
Since gcd(7,11)=1, 2 is reachable; repeatedly pour 7 into 11 and reset to step the remainder.
Track states (a,b) and count moves to first reach a 2 in some vessel.
E[TвӮҒвӮҖвӮҖ]=100В·250=25000.
Markov: P(T>26000) вүӨ E[T]/26000.
solution: Each block has mean $250$, so $\mathbb{E}[T_{100}]=25000$. By Markov's inequality, $P(T_{100}>26000)\le\dfrac{25000}{26000}=\dfrac{25}{26}.$

hints: Stop when the running sum is even; condition on the first roll's parity.
Even first (1/2): done in 1. Odd first (1/2): need a later odd to flip parity, Geom(1/2) more rolls (mean 2).
solution: If the first roll is even (prob $\tfrac12$) the sum is even and we stop. If odd, we need a subsequent odd roll to make the sum even, which takes a further $\mathrm{Geom}(\tfrac12)$ rolls (mean $2$). So $E=\tfrac12(1)+\tfrac12(1+2)=2.$

hints: Lap rates: 1/6 and 1/9 laps per minute.
Lapping = gaining a full lap at the relative rate 1/6 вҲ’ 1/9 = 1/18.
Time = 1/(1/18) = 18 minutes.
solution: Lauda gains on Hunt at $\tfrac16-\tfrac19=\tfrac1{18}$ lap per minute. To gain a full lap takes $\dfrac{1}{1/18}=18$ minutes.

hints: This is a growing perpetuity: value = CF/(r вҲ’ g).
Payout = E[max of n U(500,1000)] вҲ’ 5n = 500 + 500n/(n+1) вҲ’ 5n.
Maximize: derivative 500/(n+1)ВІ вҲ’ 5 = 0 вҮ’ (n+1)ВІ=100 вҮ’ n=9.
solution: With $n$ bidders, $\mathbb{E}[\max]=500+\tfrac{500n}{n+1}$, so the payout is $f(n)=1000-\tfrac{500}{n+1}-5n$. Setting $f'(n)=\tfrac{500}{(n+1)^2}-5=0$ gives $n+1=10$, $n=9$, and $f(9)=1000-50-45=905.$

hints: P(no boat in an hour)=1вҲ’0.737856=0.262144=0.8вҒ¶.
For a Poisson process, P(none in 10 min)=P(none in hour)^{1/6}=0.8.
P(вүҘ1 in 10 min)=1вҲ’0.8=0.2.
solution: $P(\text{no boat in an hour})=1-0.737856=0.262144=0.8^6$. Over $10$ minutes ($\tfrac16$ hour) the no-boat probability is $0.8^{6\cdot(1/6)}=0.8$, so $P(\ge1\text{ boat in 10 min})=1-0.8=0.2.$

hints: Y=IВ·X with I~Bernoulli(1/2) (heads) and X independent (mean 12, var 2).
solution: $Y=IX$ with $I\sim\mathrm{Bernoulli}(\tfrac12)$ independent of $X$ ($\mathbb{E}[X]=12$, $\mathrm{Var}(X)=2$). Then $\mathbb{E}[Y]=\tfrac12\cdot12=6$ and $\mathbb{E}[Y^2]=\tfrac12\,\mathbb{E}[X^2]=\tfrac12(2+144)=73$, so $\mathrm{Var}(Y)=73-36=37.$

hints: Total money is always 8.96. The i-th flipper, just after paying out, holds 1.28/2^{7вҲ’i}; before flipping he holds 4.48+0.64/2^{7вҲ’i}.
Earlier heads double his initial, so x_i=(4.48+0.64/2^{7вҲ’i})/2^{iвҲ’1}.
solution: The pot is always $\$8.96$. Player $i$ (the $i$-th to flip), right after paying everyone, holds $\tfrac{1.28}{2^{7-i}}$, so before flipping he holds $S_i=4.48+\tfrac{0.64}{2^{7-i}}$. Since the earlier $i-1$ heads doubled his initial stake, $x_i=S_i/2^{i-1}$, giving $x=(4.49,2.25,1.13,0.57,0.29,0.15,0.08)$. Then $\sum x_i^2=26.9374$, so $10000\sum x_i^2=269374.$

hints: Count digit 9 over 000вҖ“999 (1000 numbers, 3 positions).
Each position shows 9 in 1/10 of numbers: 100 times per position.
solution: Writing numbers $000$вҖ“$999$, each of the $3$ digit positions is a $9$ in exactly $\tfrac1{10}$ of the $1000$ numbers, i.e. $100$ times. Total $3\cdot100=300$, and $1000$ contributes no $9$s, so $300.$

hints: Alice wins iff A > max(BвӮҒ,BвӮӮ); ties go to Bob. P(max<a)=[P(B<a)]ВІ.
Sum (1/30)ОЈ_{a=1}^{30}[P(B<a)]ВІ, with P(B<a)=(aвҲ’1)/20 for aвүӨ21 and 1 beyond.
solution: Alice wins iff her roll beats both of Bob's, $A>\max(B_1,B_2)$, with $P(\max<a)=\big(P(B<a)\big)^2$. Then $P(W)=\tfrac1{30}\sum_{a=1}^{30}\big(P(B<a)\big)^2$ where $P(B<a)=\tfrac{a-1}{20}$ for $a\le21$ and $1$ for $a>20$. The sum is $\tfrac1{400}\sum_{k=0}^{19}k^2+10=6.175+10=\tfrac{647}{40}$, so $P(W)=\tfrac1{30}\cdot\tfrac{647}{40}=\tfrac{647}{1200}.$
